title: Lifecycle Vocabulary Registry
description: Normalized lifecycle state vocabulary across all domains

canonical.canonical_definition: The authoritative, ratified form of a term, schema, or implementation

supported.canonical_definition: Full runtime generation capability exists and is production-ready

semantic_only.canonical_definition: Schema is valid but no runtime topology or execution capability exists
semantic_only.runtime_meaning: Schema validation passes; no output generated
semantic_only.reconciliation_note: semantic_only implies valid schema; unsupported_runtime implies rejection

prototype.canonical_definition: Experimental implementation with relaxed validation, not production-ready
prototype.runtime_meaning: G0 acceptable, warnings allowed, clear prototype marking

partial_prototype.canonical_definition: Some features supported with warnings, others skipped

unsupported_runtime.canonical_definition: Cannot generate output, clean rejection required

research_required.canonical_definition: Future capability needed, implementation not yet available

governed.canonical_definition: Subject to governance constraints and lifecycle rules

governed_experimental.canonical_definition: Subject to governance but explicitly experimental, may change without notice
governed_experimental.runtime_meaning: Governance applies but stability not guaranteed

experimental.canonical_definition: Not yet governed, subject to change or removal

quarantine.canonical_definition: Isolated from production paths pending review or remediation
quarantine.runtime_meaning: Must not reach production without explicit release

validation_only.canonical_definition: Performs validation without producing output or modifying state

blocking.canonical_definition: Failure severity that prevents operation from proceeding

major.canonical_definition: Failure severity requiring investigation, tier-dependent handling

warning.canonical_definition: Minor issue, output usable but not ideal

known_conflicts.nature: semantic_only implies valid schema; unsupported may imply invalid schema
known_conflicts.resolution: Use semantic_only when schema validates; use unsupported when schema itself is invalid

known_conflicts.nature: Different domains use different terminology for similar concepts
known_conflicts.resolution: supported is cad_semantics vocabulary; supported_prototype is topology_builder vocabulary
